LHX.sentence: L3Harris has drifted down while other defense companies rose, which is a bit odd given that military spending keeps flowing steadily — nothing has actually changed about the business.

ASML.sentence: ASML slipped a little this week, but they're still the only company on earth that makes the machines needed to print the most advanced computer chips, so the long-term story is intact.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet stretch — they supply oxygen, nitrogen and other gases under long contracts and nudge prices up a little each quarter, which is basically the whole story.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft has fallen sharply from its high — a surprising drop for such a steady, profitable company — even as its cloud business keeps powering the AI boom in the background.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher, which sells the equipment and supplies that nearly every research and biotech lab depends on, is having a calm week with the business ticking along normally.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge, the Canadian pipeline company that earns tolls on the oil and gas it moves, is steady, though the generous dividend has a thinner-than-usual safety margin right now.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow bounced a bit today, and even though it's fallen a long way from its high, corporate and government customers keep signing on for its workflow and AI tools with no sign of trouble in the actual business.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore, the big miner and commodity trader, is in a low patch for profits right now, which for a boom-and-bust business like this often marks the bottom of the cycle rather than trouble.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin slipped again and is well below its recent trend, so this fund is going through a rough, cooling-off patch.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA, the big Latin American Coke bottler, is steady — most of its money is earned in Mexican pesos, and the peso is sitting in a normal range, so nothing dramatic is happening.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras had a good day, and its hefty dividend looks well-supported at today's oil prices, which is really the main reason to hold this Brazilian oil company.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish container-shipping giant that moves goods around the world by sea, dipped slightly and is now trading below the value of its own fleet — a sign it's cheap for this point in the shipping cycle.

MA.sentence: Mastercard ticked up and looks reasonably priced against its own history, with people continuing to tap and swipe their cards more and more every day.

ISRG.sentence: Intuitive Surgical, maker of the da Vinci operating robot, is well off its high and lagging other medical-device makers, but there's no evidence hospitals are actually using its machines any less.
